title: Jed
description: The band's raw debut on Metal Blade Records, heavily influenced by The Replacements and punk rock, establishing their scrappy Buffalo identity.

title: Hold Me Up
description: Their second album continued the punk-pop formula with tighter songwriting and energetic performances, gaining the band a loyal regional following.

title: Superstar Car Wash
description: Their major-label debut on Warner Bros. marked the beginning of their transition from punk to alternative rock, featuring more polished production.

title: A Boy Named Goo
description: The breakthrough album that went double platinum, driven by the hit single 'Name' which reached #5 on the Billboard Hot 100.

title: Dizzy Up the Girl
description: Their commercial peak, reaching quadruple platinum status with iconic singles 'Iris,' 'Slide,' 'Black Balloon,' and 'Broadway.'

title: Gutterflower
description: A platinum-selling album featuring 'Here Is Gone' and 'Sympathy,' maintaining the band's mainstream presence in the early 2000s.

title: Let Love In
description: Featured the hits 'Better Days,' 'Give a Little Bit,' and 'Stay with You,' showcasing Rzeznik's gift for anthemic, emotionally resonant rock.

title: Something for the Rest of Us
description: A darker, more introspective album exploring themes of resilience and hope in difficult times, with a more stripped-back production approach.

title: Magnetic
description: A sonic departure featuring synth-driven production and electronic textures layered over the band's guitar-driven foundation.

title: Boxes
description: Continued the band's modern production evolution with singles 'So Alive' and 'Over and Over,' exploring themes of identity and belonging.

title: Miracle Pill
description: The band's twelfth studio album blended anthemic rock with introspective ballads, demonstrating the continued evolution of Rzeznik's songwriting.

title: Chaos in Bloom
description: Self-produced by Rzeznik for the first time, this album embraces a rawer, guitar-forward sound reminiscent of the band's earlier work.
